title: Customer and payment method sharing
description: Test how customers and payment methods work across accounts in your sandbox organization. Follow the steps below to create a shared customer, save a card, and charge it from a different account.

steps.title: Enable customer and payment method sharing
steps.description: Enable sharing for both accounts in your sandbox organization. This creates a sharing group so customers and card payment methods are available across accounts.

nodes.title: Enable sharing in organization settings
nodes.description: Navigate to your organization sharing settings and create a sharing group that includes both accounts. This step auto-completes once sharing is verified between the two accounts.
nodes.review_prompt: Open Dashboard and confirm the required configuration is present before continuing.

steps.title: Create a customer and save a card
steps.description: Create a customer in Account 1 and save a card payment method through checkout. This is the data that will be shared to Account 2.

nodes.title: Create a shared customer in Account 1
nodes.description: Use the org-level API key with the Stripe-Context header to create the customer in Account 1. This customer will be available to Account 2 once sharing is enabled.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Save a card in Account 1
nodes.description: Create a Checkout Session for $10 in Account 1 with the customer attached. The payment method will be saved to the customer for future use.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Complete checkout in Account 1
nodes.description: Complete the Checkout Session. This charges the customer $10 and saves the card as a reusable payment method on the shared customer.
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the user-facing flow works as described.

steps.title: Charge the shared card from Account 2
steps.description: Use the shared customer and saved payment method from Account 1 to complete a payment in Account 2. This confirms sharing works across your accounts.

nodes.title: Charge the shared card in Account 2
nodes.description: Create a Checkout Session for $20 in Account 2 using the shared customer. The payment method saved from the first checkout will be reused.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Complete checkout in Account 2
nodes.description: Complete the Checkout Session using the payment method saved to the shared customer from Account 1. The shared card works—you're ready to enable sharing in production.
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the user-facing flow works as described.
